mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, increasing present values of future corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in over 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer demand and residential investment, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive movement of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with a combined Rank IC below 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained supply-side capacity and rising input costs, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles do not precede negative 20-day returns, or the predictive accuracy drops below 45% in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between the federal funds rate and the consumer price index indicates real interest rates are rising faster than price pressures, improving corporate profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: The FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL spread shows no predictive relationship with 5-day forward returns, or positive spreads consistently coincide with negative market moves.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls reflect weakening economic activity and reduced corporate revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with a walk-forward Rank IC remaining below 0.03 across all tested macro environments.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% confirms robust aggregate demand and expanding corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive when GDPC1 growth surpasses 2.5%, or the signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial production and rising unemployment during a recessionary phase signals severe supply-demand mismatch and liquidity stress, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-UNRATE divergence does not predict negative 5-day returns during recessions, or the mechanism fails to achieve a positive information coefficient in regime-filtered samples.
